response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of strong, varied software engineering experience and genuine developer-tooling depth — test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD and GitLab infrastructure setup, and MCP/agent tooling — which maps well to the engineering-productivity technical focus. However, the resume shows an exclusively individual-contributor trajectory (Staff/Senior Engineer roles) with no evidence of formal people management, performance reviews, one-on-ones, or leading organizational change, which are core requirements for this people-leadership role. Mobile-platform exposure is also thin (a single iOS/Apple TV stint). A conversation could clarify any informal leadership, but the management gap is significant for a Level II Manager position.
strengths: Deep developer-tools experience: test runners, assertion libs, CI/CD, build infra
15+ years across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong engineering-craft mindset (TDD, pairing, trunk-based dev) and teaching/mentoring via workshops
concerns: No demonstrated people-management or team-leadership experience (core requirement)
No evidence of leading organizational change or owning team roadmaps
Limited mobile-platform depth for a major mobile ecosystem focus
